items.question: Which statement best matches the broad definition of AI in this roadmap?
options: AI is only deep neural networks trained on internet-scale data
AI is the field of systems that perceive, reason, learn, and act toward goals
AI is any software that runs without human clicks
AI is identical to generative chatbots
items.answer: AI is the field of systems that perceive, reason, learn, and act toward goals
items.explanation: The broad definition covers rules, search, ML, and more—not only neural generative models.

items.question: Machine learning is best described as:
options: A synonym for all of AI with no exceptions
A subset of AI where systems learn patterns from data
A replacement for robotics and planning
items.answer: A subset of AI where systems learn patterns from data
items.explanation: ML ⊆ AI. Sibling topic Machine Learning owns the canonical ML definition.

items.question: Which is an example of narrow AI (not AGI)?
options: A hypothetical system that masters every profession instantly
A fraud classifier scoring transactions in real time
An omniscient oracle with no task bounds
items.answer: A fraud classifier scoring transactions in real time
items.explanation: Narrow AI targets a specific task with measurable performance; AGI is not deployed.

items.question: A chess engine using minimax search and hand-tuned evaluation is:
options: Not AI because it does not use ChatGPT
AI but not necessarily machine learning
items.answer: AI but not necessarily machine learning
items.explanation: Search and planning are classic AI; learning from data is not required for AI status.
